title: Ctenitis setosa
intro: Ctenitis setosa is a species of fern, specifically a member of the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, Ctenitis setosa is used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis setosa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. In herbal medicine, Ctenitis setosa is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tea, and it is also used in the form of capsules or tablets for its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Ctenitis setosa is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. This plant is a member of the genus Ctenitis, a genus of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species Ctenitis setosa is further divided into two subspecies, Ctenitis setosa subsp. setosa and Ctenitis setosa subsp. glaberrima. Ctenitis setosa is a species of tree fern that is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Indonesia and Malaysia. It is an evergreen plant that grows in the wild, often in humid and shaded environments.
